stateFamilies.label: Intake and Queue
stateFamilies.summary: The network request is being validated, policy-scoped, and admitted into the hub queue under preserved booking lineage.

stateFamilies.label: Coordination
stateFamilies.summary: Ownership, candidate discovery, and coordinator decision-making are active inside the hub shell.

stateFamilies.label: Patient Choice
stateFamilies.summary: A governed offer pack exists, the full open-choice set is visible, and stale mutations are blocked by tuple drift.

stateFamilies.label: Commit and Truth
stateFamilies.summary: A selected candidate is revalidated, committed, and then held in acknowledgement debt until origin-practice visibility is satisfied.

stateFamilies.label: Fallback and Return
stateFamilies.summary: The hub has switched from direct coordination to callback or return-to-practice continuity, but the case stays supervised until linkage is durable.

stateFamilies.label: Completion
stateFamilies.summary: Closure is permitted only after ownership, truth, fallback, and acknowledgement blockers are all clear.

states.label: Hub requested
states.summary: The booking branch has asked for hub coordination, but the network request has not yet been validated into a queue-admissible child lineage.
states.entryPredicateSummary: A NetworkBookingRequest exists for the same requestLineageRef, origin booking refs are preserved, and the hub child link has not yet been admitted into active queue posture.
states.routeCue: Read-only provenance in the same shell with a lineage mini-diagram and no live coordination actions.
truthNotes: Operational state only; no patient or practice reassurance may be inferred.
Hub entry cannot overwrite BookingCase.lineageCaseLinkRef.

states.label: Intake validated
states.summary: The hub handoff has passed lineage, origin-practice, and policy admission checks and is ready to join the queue.
states.entryPredicateSummary: Lineage, origin booking, patient constraints, and reason-for-routing fields are complete, and the current policy evaluation admits the case to hub coordination.
states.routeCue: Queue-ready card inside the hub shell with explicit origin practice and routing summary.
truthNotes: Still not patient-visible booked truth.
Queue posture is independent from future offer and confirmation truth.

states.label: Queued
states.summary: The case is in the hub work queue but not yet actively claimed by one coordinator lease.
states.entryPredicateSummary: Queue entry time is recorded, ownership is unclaimed, and the same-shell queue family is the authoritative view of the active work item.
states.routeCue: Queue lane row with claim, monitor, or supervised return controls.
truthNotes: Queue admission does not imply candidate search has started.
Escalated return remains legal directly from queue if policy or urgency requires it.

states.label: Claimed
states.summary: One coordinator holds the current ownership fence and may advance the case under the active acting context.
states.entryPredicateSummary: Claim succeeded under the current acting scope, ownership fence token, and visibility contract, and the case is now writable by exactly one active lease.
states.routeCue: Case console with live DecisionDock and coordination timers.
truthNotes: Claimed status is operational ownership only.
No candidate or offer truth is implied yet.

states.label: Candidate searching
states.summary: The coordinator is resolving or refreshing the clinically acceptable, trusted frontier under the current policy tuple.
states.entryPredicateSummary: One active ownership fence exists and the current policy evaluation allows candidate discovery against trusted capacity sources.
states.routeCue: Case console with candidate refresh in progress and stale tuple guardrails visible.
truthNotes: Candidate search is not patient-choice posture.
Degraded or quarantined sources may inform diagnostics only, never patient offerability.

states.label: Candidates ready
states.summary: The current candidate snapshot and ranked plan are stable enough for coordinator review or alternative packaging.
states.entryPredicateSummary: A fresh candidate snapshot, rank proof, and current policy tuple all agree on the same offerable frontier for this case version.
states.routeCue: Case console with ranked candidates and explicit choose-offer-or-return posture.
truthNotes: Candidates-ready remains pre-selection.
Any future patient-choice route must bind the same offer set hash and truth tuple.

states.label: Coordinator selecting
states.summary: A coordinator is choosing a direct candidate or moving the case into a governed alternative-offer flow.
states.entryPredicateSummary: Exactly one governing case, one active acting context, one policy tuple, and one candidate plan are current for the selection action.
states.routeCue: DecisionDock prioritizes commit, offer, callback, or return while keeping the case pulse fixed.
truthNotes: Selection is not commit.
Callback and return transitions are first-class legal exits, not exception-only side paths.

states.label: Alternatives offered
states.summary: A real AlternativeOfferSession exists and the full patient-offerable set is packaged under one offerSetHash.
states.entryPredicateSummary: A governed offer pack exists, callback remains a separate fallback card, and the active truth projection blocks close by offer.
states.routeCue: Alternative-offer child route inside the same hub shell with pinned provenance and no hidden top-K funnel.
truthNotes: Offer recommendation is advisory only.
Offer availability does not imply booking confirmation or practice visibility.

states.label: Patient choice pending
states.summary: At least one live alternative has been delivered and the case is now waiting for patient choice, decline, callback, expiry, or regeneration.
states.entryPredicateSummary: Offer delivery or phone read-back has started, patient-visible choice state is live, and callback is still modeled outside the ranked options.
states.routeCue: Same-shell offer route with live patient-choice posture and stale mutation blocking.
truthNotes: Choice pending is still open-choice, not commit.
Expiry, embedded drift, or subject drift must preserve provenance but disable stale accept and callback actions.

states.label: Candidate revalidating
states.summary: The chosen candidate is being rechecked against live capacity, source version, reservation fence, and current policy outputs.
states.dominantAuthority: HubCommitAttempt preflight + reservation fence + current policy evaluation
states.entryPredicateSummary: A chosen candidate exists, current tuples still match, and the system is executing the last safe check before any native side effect occurs.
states.routeCue: Case console or commit route with selected candidate pinned and fresh revalidation blockers visible.
truthNotes: Revalidation failure returns the case to search or choice, not quiet failure.
No booked or calm manage posture is legal here.

states.label: Native booking pending
states.summary: A fenced HubCommitAttempt is in flight and may be the only object allowed to talk to the native booking surface.
states.entryPredicateSummary: Revalidation passed, a commit intent was durably journaled, and any external side effect is bound to one idempotent attempt.
states.routeCue: Commit route with authoritative pending wording and no false exclusivity claims.
truthNotes: Pending native booking is not confirmation.
Partial failure must route to reconciliation, not a second parallel attempt.

states.label: Confirmation pending
states.summary: Some booking proof exists, but the monotone confirmation gate has not yet cleared final booked truth.
states.entryPredicateSummary: The active commit attempt has not yet met hard-match and confidence thresholds, so only pending confirmation posture is legal.
states.routeCue: Pending confirmation strip with explicit proof status, not calm appointment reassurance.
truthNotes: Patient confirmation may be provisional only.
Practice acknowledgement cannot start from stale or disputed truth.

states.label: Booked pending practice acknowledgement
states.summary: Authoritative hub confirmation exists, but the origin practice still owes acknowledgement for the current generation.
states.entryPredicateSummary: The appointment is confirmed for the active tuple, patient-facing confirmation may render, and ackGeneration has been minted but not yet satisfied.
states.routeCue: Case stays in active oversight with current acknowledgement debt, due time, and continuity messaging evidence.
truthNotes: Patient confirmation and practice acknowledgement are orthogonal facets.
Transport acceptance alone may not clear acknowledgement debt.

states.label: Booked
states.summary: Confirmed hub appointment truth and current-generation practice acknowledgement both exist for the live truth tuple.
states.entryPredicateSummary: Authoritative confirmation, current tuple agreement, and current-generation practice acknowledgement all align.
states.routeCue: Booked case summary with closure eligibility explicitly tied to open blocker emptiness.
truthNotes: Booked still is not closed.
Close remains a LifecycleCoordinator decision gated by empty blocker set.

states.label: Callback transfer pending
states.summary: Callback fallback has been chosen, but the callback case or expectation envelope is not yet durably linked and patient-visible.
states.entryPredicateSummary: The fallback card or callback path was selected, but callback continuity is not yet durable for the current fence and lineage.
states.routeCue: Same-shell fallback card stays visible as provenance while linkage completes.
truthNotes: The prior offer context remains visible as read-only provenance.
This state exists to prevent silent callback handoff gaps.

states.label: Callback offered
states.summary: Callback linkage is durable and the patient-facing CallbackExpectationEnvelope is now the governing continuity object.
states.entryPredicateSummary: The linked callback workflow and expectation envelope exist and are current for the same request lineage and fallback fence.
states.routeCue: Read-only callback provenance inside the same shell until the hub branch can legally close.
truthNotes: Callback-offered is not a hidden close.
Hub tooling may observe or repair callback continuity, but may not reinterpret the patient state from stale local waitlist truth.

states.label: Escalated back
states.summary: The case has been returned to origin practice or duty-clinician workflow with preserved urgency carry and linked return proof.
states.entryPredicateSummary: No clinically acceptable trusted offer exists, or the lead-time inequality fails, and return workflow linkage has been created or is being finalized.
states.routeCue: Urgent return panel stays in the same case console with rationale and linked practice task preview.
truthNotes: Escalated-back is a durable operational branch state, not an exception note.
The practice task must be materially linked before close can succeed.

states.label: Closed
states.summary: The hub branch is durably finished and no remaining OpenCaseBlockers(h) keep it in live oversight.
states.entryPredicateSummary: LifecycleCoordinator persisted the close decision, fallback linkage is durable, and all ownership, continuity, confirmation, and visibility blockers are clear.
states.routeCue: Audit-first review posture inside the same shell family.
truthNotes: Closed is the only legal finished state for the hub branch.
Closure may not be inferred from booked, callback, or return states alone.

truthAxes.label: Hub workflow state
truthAxes.notes: Operational state only. This must not be used as a shortcut for patient reassurance or practice visibility.

truthAxes.label: Offer to confirmation truth
truthAxes.notes: Patient-visible offer, provisional receipt, booked truth, practice visibility, and closeability all derive here.

truthAxes.label: Ownership lease and fence
truthAxes.notes: All writable commands require the current fence token and epoch.

truthAxes.label: Acting scope and visibility
truthAxes.notes: Cross-org access is a first-class command envelope, not a UI-local role check.

transitions.predicate: NetworkBookingRequest exists, origin booking refs are preserved, and a child hub LineageCaseLink has been created under the origin booking link for the same request lineage.
transitions.truthPredicate: The booking branch remains the parent lineage owner and the hub branch is admitted as a child, not a replacement.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Current NetworkCoordinationPolicyEvaluation admits the case to hub workflow and queue entry metadata is persisted.
transitions.truthPredicate: Queue posture is legal only after lineaged intake validation and policy admission both hold.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: A current ownership lease, fence token, and epoch have been minted for one acting context under the current visibility contract.
transitions.truthPredicate: Only one live claim may hold the writable fence for the hub case.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Policy, urgency carry, or supervisor decision requires direct return before active coordination starts, and the return workflow is being or has been linked.
transitions.truthPredicate: Return is lawful directly from queue when hub coordination itself is not the safe continuation.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: A coordinator with the current ownership fence requests or resumes candidate discovery under the current policy tuple.
transitions.truthPredicate: Search starts only under one current acting scope and fence pair.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Policy or patient preference requires callback immediately and the callback continuity objects have not yet been durably linked.
transitions.truthPredicate: Callback transfer remains open until linkage is durable.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Direct return to practice or supervised back-escalation is required before discovery proceeds.
transitions.truthPredicate: Hub coordination may fail safely before candidate search starts.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: One current candidate snapshot and cross-site decision plan agree on a trusted, policy-valid frontier.
transitions.truthPredicate: The active policy tuple and rank proof still name the same candidates.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The search result shows callback is the only safe continuation and callback linkage is not yet durable.
transitions.truthPredicate: Degraded or no-slot discovery may choose callback, but only through the governed fallback branch.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: No clinically acceptable trusted frontier remains or lead-time policy fails, so return-to-practice linkage must govern.
transitions.truthPredicate: No-slot return is a governed continuation, not an implicit search failure.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: A current candidate frontier exists and the coordinator is choosing direct commit versus alternative packaging under one acting scope and tuple.
transitions.truthPredicate: Selection remains in the case shell and may not infer permission from cached rows or frontend-only state.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: A real AlternativeOfferSession plus optimisation plan has been persisted from the current clinically acceptable trusted frontier.
transitions.truthPredicate: Patient choice may start only from a governed offer session, not raw top-K rows.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Callback is the selected continuation and the linked callback objects are not yet durable.
transitions.truthPredicate: Fallback stays explicit and open until linked.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Return-to-practice becomes the safe continuation before any selection or offer packaging completes.
transitions.truthPredicate: Escalation remains a direct governed branch from candidates-ready.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The offer set is actually delivered or a structured phone read-back session begins for the current offerSetHash.
transitions.truthPredicate: Patient choice opens only after deliverable offer posture exists.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The offer pack is not delivered as the active branch and the coordinator resumes direct candidate selection under the same current tuple.
transitions.truthPredicate: Choice packaging may return to coordinator selection without pretending delivery occurred.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The separate AlternativeOfferFallbackCard was selected and callback linkage is not yet durable.
transitions.truthPredicate: Callback remains outside ranked slot ordinals and becomes its own branch state.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Offer generation proves no safe or lawful patient-offerable path remains, so return-to-practice takes over.
transitions.truthPredicate: Generated offer provenance may remain visible, but active workflow changes to return.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The patient or assisted read-back path explicitly selected one current offer entry and it still matches the active offerSetHash and truthTupleHash.
transitions.truthPredicate: Choice acceptance must still re-enter coordinator or commit posture under the same current truth tuple.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The patient explicitly requests callback from the separate fallback card and callback linkage remains pending.
transitions.truthPredicate: Choice does not silently collapse into callback; the fallback link must still be durable.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Offer expiry, regenerated supply, or lead-time exhaustion makes return-to-practice the only safe continuation.
transitions.truthPredicate: Expired or drifted offer sets preserve provenance only and cannot stay quietly actionable.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Exactly one governing case, selected candidate, acting scope, and policy tuple are current, so revalidation may begin.
transitions.truthPredicate: Commit may start only from a persisted selection, never from UI-local highlight state.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The coordinator intentionally chooses callback and linkage is not yet durable.
transitions.truthPredicate: Coordinator choice still routes through governed callback continuity.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The coordinator or supervisor chooses return-to-practice and the return workflow is being linked.
transitions.truthPredicate: Return remains a first-class controlled branch from coordinator selection.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Selected candidate, source version, policy tuple, and reservation fence still align, and commit intent has been durably journaled.
transitions.truthPredicate: Native booking begins only after successful revalidation against the current tuple.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The direct candidate became stale or unavailable, but a fresh governed alternative offer set remains clinically acceptable.
transitions.truthPredicate: Direct commit failure may reopen governed open choice rather than loose local search.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The candidate can no longer be booked safely and callback continuity is now the only lawful continuation, pending durable linkage.
transitions.truthPredicate: Revalidation failure may move to callback only through the governed fallback branch.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The candidate is stale, unavailable, policy-invalid, or trust-invalid and no governed offer or callback path remains sufficient.
transitions.truthPredicate: Unsafe revalidation outcomes must route to governed return, not silent retry.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The native or manual path produced provisional proof, but the confirmation gate has not yet passed hard-match and confidence thresholds.
transitions.truthPredicate: Pending confirmation is the only legal state when booked truth is not yet authoritative.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Authoritative hub confirmation exists for the active attempt and current tuple, and the new ackGeneration has been minted for origin-practice visibility.
transitions.truthPredicate: Booked truth may advance before practice acknowledgement only into the explicit pending-ack state.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Commit outcome or external uncertainty forces callback continuity and the callback linkage is not yet durable.
transitions.truthPredicate: Commit failure recovery remains same-shell and governed.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Commit outcome, policy drift, or unresolved supplier truth requires explicit return-to-practice continuity.
transitions.truthPredicate: Commit-side recovery may not bypass governed return linkage.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The active confirmation gate now passes all hard matches for the current tuple and the appointment plus practice continuity message are durably committed.
transitions.truthPredicate: Pending confirmation becomes booked-pending-ack only after authoritative confirmation and new practice visibility generation exist together.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Confirmation ambiguity or expiry forces callback continuity and callback linkage is still pending.
transitions.truthPredicate: Callback remains explicit recovery from pending confirmation.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Confirmation dispute or expiry requires return-to-practice continuity and the return branch is being or has been linked.
transitions.truthPredicate: Pending confirmation cannot silently die; it returns through a governed branch.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Current-generation acknowledgement evidence or audited no-ack-required policy exception satisfies the live ackGeneration and truth tuple.
transitions.truthPredicate: Only current-generation evidence may clear practice acknowledgement debt.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Practice visibility or continuity risk requires governed return or supervised escalation before booked closure can proceed.
transitions.truthPredicate: Unmet acknowledgement duty may reopen escalation instead of falsely completing the case.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: OpenCaseBlockers(h) is empty and LifecycleCoordinator persisted the governing close decision.
transitions.truthPredicate: Booked truth alone never closes the case; close needs empty blockers and coordinator decision proof.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: The active HubFallbackRecord is now linked to the current CallbackCase and a fresh CallbackExpectationEnvelope for the same fallback fence.
transitions.truthPredicate: Callback-offered is legal only after durable callback linkage.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Callback is no longer sufficient or lawful and return-to-practice becomes the governing continuation.
transitions.truthPredicate: Callback-pending may still switch to governed return without hidden loss of provenance.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Callback continuity is durable, fallback state is transferred or completed, and no other open blocker remains.
transitions.truthPredicate: Hub close waits for durable callback linkage, not just callback selection.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

transitions.predicate: Return-to-practice workflow is durably linked on the request lineage, fallback state is transferred or completed, and open blockers are empty.
transitions.truthPredicate: Escalated-back remains open until the linked return workflow is real and current.
transitions.sourceRef: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine

openCaseBlockersRule.definition: OpenCaseBlockers(h) is the set of active refs among live ownership lease, live HubOwnershipTransition, every blockingRef named by current HubOfferToConfirmationTruthProjection, unresolved supplier drift, unresolved callback expectation publication, continuity-evidence blocks on writable routes, and any active identity repair disposition.

sourceRefs: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5A. Network coordination contract, case model, and state machine
